summary: Small header documentation fix in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/pobj.h`: the inline comment describing `HSD_PObj::display` was corrected and moved to a Doxygen-style `///` comment. The displayed primitive layout now documents `vtxcnt` as `u16` instead of `u8`, and marks the embedded types with `#` references.
what_changed: Changed the documented display stream layout from `u8 primitive, u8 vtxcnt, u16* indices` to `#u8 primitive, #u16 vtxcnt, #u16* indices`.
Moved the layout note from a trailing inline comment on `u8* display;` to a preceding `///` documentation comment.
PR body says this resolves issue #995.
smart_moves: Corrected a misleading type in a struct field comment without changing code layout or ABI-sensitive declarations.
Used a doc-comment form that keeps the field declaration clean while preserving important binary-format information near the field.
Added `#` markers before type names, likely aligning the comment with project documentation conventions for symbol/type references.
decomp_lessons: Header comments describing packed or streamed data layouts matter: an incorrect width such as `u8` vs `u16` can mislead future struct reconstruction and parser work.
For pointer fields that reference embedded serialized data rather than a normal C array, document the pointed-to layout explicitly next to the field.
Documentation-only fixes can still be valuable in decompilation projects when they prevent incorrect assumptions about binary data formats.
Avoid changing declarations for comment-only corrections unless there is direct evidence that the struct type itself is wrong.
naming_conventions: `HSD_PObj` names a SysDolphin/Baselib polygon object struct.
`HSD_VtxDescList`, `HSD_JObj`, and `HSD_ShapeSet` retain the `HSD_` prefix convention for HAL/System Dolphin types.
`n_display` follows the existing count-field naming pattern.
`shape_set` uses snake_case for the union member corresponding to `HSD_ShapeSet`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or matching tactics were present in the PR evidence.
The change is comment-only and should not affect generated code or matching.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were included in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Doxygen type reference comments in headers
